title: toona ciliata var. australis
intro: Toona ciliata var. australis, also known as the Australian cedar or yellow mahogany, is a tree species native to eastern Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and protecting against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations, such as tea and tinctures, can be made from the leaves and bark of Toona ciliata var. australis, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
